methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $103M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Zeitview
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Oxa is a Series D autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $103M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a narrow peer universe of five private companies (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, Zeitview) with no public comparables available in the same stage and valuation tier.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and forms the sole quantitative anchor for this analysis. Using total capital raised ($103M) as a proxy for pre-money valuation and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $154M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow filtration criteria; while four same-sector peers exist, none met minimum thresholds for statistical validity, and sector-wide medians would introduce unacceptable measurement error.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $131M (low) to $178M (high), centered at $154M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result, acknowledging both the single-method constraint and inherent uncertainty in private frontier-tech valuations where public benchmarking is absent. Key uncertainties include the durability of the 1.5x multiplier in a tightening capital market and the absence of revenue or margin data to validate pricing relative to fundamental metrics. A future round or material operational milestone would meaningfully refine this estimate.
